Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|12=draft}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=draft}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}}.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|12=draft}} further indicate the influence of the reign of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|12=draft}}. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}}, leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Motoyama{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} | part_of = {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Somanochi Town, Mine-dō{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}} | coordinates = 34| coordinates = 35| coordinates = 38.25 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 50| coordinates = 57.27 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Circular mound | area = Diameter 35.5m
Height 5m | burial = {{ill|Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Iwayayama type) | burial goods = | Associated Mounds = | built = Early to mid-7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb = | Historic Site = None | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = Circular mound | map_dot_label = Minezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = Top }} '''Minezuka Kofun''' (峯塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. The Kofun, shaped as a circular mound, is part of the {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} and is not designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The kofun is a large circular Kofun built at the southern foot of a small hill ridge extending east from the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}.Explanatory board at the site. A survey was conducted by the {{ill|Tenri University History Research Society|ja|天理大学歴史研究会|12=draft}} in 1969, but no excavation has been carried out to date. The mound is circular, built in three tiers. The first tier measures 35.5 meters in diameter, the second tier 28.4 meters, and the third tier 17.6 meters. The mound stands approximately 5 meters tall. The outer surface of the mound features rectangular blocks of tuff sandstone (Tenri sandstone), giving it a brick-like appearance. There is also speculation about the presence of a surrounding moat. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. This meticulously constructed large chamber uses cut stones of granite, resembling the chamber of {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka Village|ja|明日香村|12=draft}} and is classified as an Iwayayama-type chamber. However, the burial goods remain unclear due to early looting. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}. It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=draft}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|12=draft}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=draft}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}}.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|12=draft}} further indicate the influence of the reign of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|12=draft}}. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}}, leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - The mound is circular, built in three tiers. The first tier measures 35.5 meters in diameter, the second tier 28.4 meters, and the third tier 17.6 meters. The mound stands approximately 5 meters tall. The outer surface of the mound features rectangular blocks of tuff sandstone (Tenri sandstone), giving it a brick-like appearance. There is also speculation about the presence of a surrounding moat. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. This meticulously constructed large chamber uses cut stones of granite, resembling the chamber of {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka Village|ja|明日香村|12=draft}} and is classified as an Iwayayama-type chamber. However, the burial goods remain unclear due to early looting. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}. It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=draft}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|12=draft}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=draft}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}}.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|12=draft}} further indicate the influence of the reign of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|12=draft}}. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}}, leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Motoyama{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} | part_of = {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Somanochi Town, Mine-dō{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}} | coordinates = 34| coordinates = 35| coordinates = 38.25 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 50| coordinates = 57.27 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Circular mound | area = Diameter 35.5m
Height 5m | burial = {{ill|Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Iwayayama type) | burial goods = | Associated Mounds = | built = Early to mid-7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb = | Historic Site = None | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = Circular mound | map_dot_label = Minezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = Top }} '''Minezuka Kofun''' (峯塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. The Kofun, shaped as a circular mound, is part of the {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} and is not designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The kofun is a large circular Kofun built at the southern foot of a small hill ridge extending east from the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}.Explanatory board at the site. A survey was conducted by the {{ill|Tenri University History Research Society|ja|天理大学歴史研究会|12=draft}} in 1969, but no excavation has been carried out to date. The mound is circular, built in three tiers. The first tier measures 35.5 meters in diameter, the second tier 28.4 meters, and the third tier 17.6 meters. The mound stands approximately 5 meters tall. The outer surface of the mound features rectangular blocks of tuff sandstone (Tenri sandstone), giving it a brick-like appearance. There is also speculation about the presence of a surrounding moat. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. This meticulously constructed large chamber uses cut stones of granite, resembling the chamber of {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka Village|ja|明日香村|12=draft}} and is classified as an Iwayayama-type chamber. However, the burial goods remain unclear due to early looting. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}. It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=draft}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|12=draft}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=draft}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}}.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|12=draft}} further indicate the influence of the reign of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|12=draft}}. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}}, leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - Tenri City Official Website {{citation needed|Minezuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanochi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Burial sites of the Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:峯塚古墳]] [[sn:Minezuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Minezuka Kofun}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Minezuka Kofun in Habikino City, Osaka Prefecture|Minekazuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}s | name = Minezuka Kofun | image = [[File:Minezuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Motoyama{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} | part_of = {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Somanochi Town, Mine-dō{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}} | coordinates = 34| coordinates = 35| coordinates = 38.25 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 50| coordinates = 57.27 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Circular mound | area = Diameter 35.5m
Height 5m | burial = {{ill|Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Iwayayama type) | burial goods = | Associated Mounds = | built = Early to mid-7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb = | Historic Site = None | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = Circular mound | map_dot_label = Minezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = Top }} '''Minezuka Kofun''' (峯塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. The Kofun, shaped as a circular mound, is part of the {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} and is not designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The kofun is a large circular Kofun built at the southern foot of a small hill ridge extending east from the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}.Explanatory board at the site. A survey was conducted by the {{ill|Tenri University History Research Society|ja|天理大学歴史研究会|12=draft}} in 1969, but no excavation has been carried out to date. The mound is circular, built in three tiers. The first tier measures 35.5 meters in diameter, the second tier 28.4 meters, and the third tier 17.6 meters. The mound stands approximately 5 meters tall. The outer surface of the mound features rectangular blocks of tuff sandstone (Tenri sandstone), giving it a brick-like appearance. There is also speculation about the presence of a surrounding moat. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. This meticulously constructed large chamber uses cut stones of granite, resembling the chamber of {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka Village|ja|明日香村|12=draft}} and is classified as an Iwayayama-type chamber. However, the burial goods remain unclear due to early looting. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}. It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=draft}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|12=draft}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=draft}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}}.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|12=draft}} further indicate the influence of the reign of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|12=draft}}. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}}, leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - Tenri City Official Website {{citation needed|Minezuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanochi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Burial sites of the Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:峯塚古墳]] [[sn:Minezuka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Minezuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=draft}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|12=draft}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=draft}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}}.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|12=draft}} further indicate the influence of the reign of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|12=draft}}. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}}, leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - Tenri City Official Website {{citation needed|Minezuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Minezuka Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Minezuka Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanochi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Burial sites of the Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:峯塚古墳]] [[sn:Minezuka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Minezuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Minezuka Kofun in Habikino City, Osaka Prefecture|Minekazuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}s | name = Minezuka Kofun | image = [[File:Minezuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Motoyama{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} | part_of = {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Somanochi Town, Mine-dō{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}} | coordinates = 34| coordinates = 35| coordinates = 38.25 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 50| coordinates = 57.27 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Circular mound | area = Diameter 35.5m
Height 5m | burial = {{ill|Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Iwayayama type) | burial goods = | Associated Mounds = | built = Early to mid-7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb = | Historic Site = None | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = Circular mound | map_dot_label = Minezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = Top }} '''Minezuka Kofun''' (峯塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. The Kofun, shaped as a circular mound, is part of the {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} and is not designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The kofun is a large circular Kofun built at the southern foot of a small hill ridge extending east from the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}.Explanatory board at the site. A survey was conducted by the {{ill|Tenri University History Research Society|ja|天理大学歴史研究会|12=draft}} in 1969, but no excavation has been carried out to date. The mound is circular, built in three tiers. The first tier measures 35.5 meters in diameter, the second tier 28.4 meters, and the third tier 17.6 meters. The mound stands approximately 5 meters tall. The outer surface of the mound features rectangular blocks of tuff sandstone (Tenri sandstone), giving it a brick-like appearance. There is also speculation about the presence of a surrounding moat. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. This meticulously constructed large chamber uses cut stones of granite, resembling the chamber of {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka Village|ja|明日香村|12=draft}} and is classified as an Iwayayama-type chamber. However, the burial goods remain unclear due to early looting. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}. It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=draft}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|12=draft}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=draft}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}}.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|12=draft}} further indicate the influence of the reign of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|12=draft}}. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}}, leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Motoyama{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} | part_of = {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Somanochi Town, Mine-dō{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}} | coordinates = 34| coordinates = 35| coordinates = 38.25 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 50| coordinates = 57.27 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Circular mound | area = Diameter 35.5m
Height 5m | burial = {{ill|Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (Iwayayama type) | burial goods = | Associated Mounds = | built = Early to mid-7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb = | Historic Site = None | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = Circular mound | map_dot_label = Minezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = Top }} '''Minezuka Kofun''' (峯塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. The Kofun, shaped as a circular mound, is part of the {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} and is not designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The kofun is a large circular Kofun built at the southern foot of a small hill ridge extending east from the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}.Explanatory board at the site. A survey was conducted by the {{ill|Tenri University History Research Society|ja|天理大学歴史研究会|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1969, but no excavation has been carried out to date. The mound is circular, built in three tiers. The first tier measures 35.5 meters in diameter, the second tier 28.4 meters, and the third tier 17.6 meters. The mound stands approximately 5 meters tall. The outer surface of the mound features rectangular blocks of tuff sandstone (Tenri sandstone), giving it a brick-like appearance. There is also speculation about the presence of a surrounding moat. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. This meticulously constructed large chamber uses cut stones of granite, resembling the chamber of {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka Village|ja|明日香村|12=draft|12=draft}} and is classified as an Iwayayama-type chamber. However, the burial goods remain unclear due to early looting. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|12=draft|12=draft}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} further indicate the influence of the reign of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - Tenri City Official Website {{citation needed|Minezuka Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanochi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Burial sites of the Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Minezuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯塚古墳]]

The chamber shares characteristics with the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Minezuka Kofun in Habikino City, Osaka Prefecture|Minekazuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}s | name = Minezuka Kofun | image = [[File:Minezuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Motoyama{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} | part_of = {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanochi Town, Mine-dō{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}} | coordinates = 34| coordinates = 35| coordinates = 38.25 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 50| coordinates = 57.27 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Circular mound | area = Diameter 35.5m
Height 5m | burial = {{ill|Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Iwayayama type) | burial goods = | Associated Mounds = | built = Early to mid-7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb = | Historic Site = None | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = Circular mound | map_dot_label = Minezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = Top }} '''Minezuka Kofun''' (峯塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. The Kofun, shaped as a circular mound, is part of the {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and is not designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The kofun is a large circular Kofun built at the southern foot of a small hill ridge extending east from the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}.Explanatory board at the site. A survey was conducted by the {{ill|Tenri University History Research Society|ja|天理大学歴史研究会}} in 1969, but no excavation has been carried out to date. The mound is circular, built in three tiers. The first tier measures 35.5 meters in diameter, the second tier 28.4 meters, and the third tier 17.6 meters. The mound stands approximately 5 meters tall. The outer surface of the mound features rectangular blocks of tuff sandstone (Tenri sandstone), giving it a brick-like appearance. There is also speculation about the presence of a surrounding moat. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. This meticulously constructed large chamber uses cut stones of granite, resembling the chamber of {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka Village|ja|明日香村}} and is classified as an Iwayayama-type chamber. However, the burial goods remain unclear due to early looting. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}. It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡}} further indicate the influence of the reign of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}}. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳}}, leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - revision.text:
{{Otheruses||大阪府羽曳野市にある古墳|峯ヶ塚古墳}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 峯塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Minezuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
石室開口部 |別名 = 墓山{{Sfn|峯塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}} |所属 = [[杣之内古墳群]] |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町字峯堂{{Sfn|峯塚古墳（天理市の文化財）|1990}} |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 38.25 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 57.27 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径35.5m
高さ5m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]（岩屋山式） |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]前半-中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 峯塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''峯塚古墳'''（みねづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[杣之内古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]東縁、東から延びる小丘陵尾根の南裾に築造された大型円墳である史跡説明板。。[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）に[[天理大学]]歴史研究会による測量調査が実施されているほか、これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形。墳丘は3段築成で、1段目裾は直径35.5メートル、2段目裾は直径28.4メートル、3段目裾は直径17.6メートルを測り、墳丘全体としては高さ約5メートルを測る。墳丘外表では[[凝灰岩]]質[[砂岩]]（天理砂岩）の長方形切石によるレンガ状貼石が認められる。また墳丘周囲には周濠の可能性が指摘される。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。[[花崗岩]]の巨石の切石を用いた整美な大型石室で、[[岩屋山古墳]]（[[明日香村]]）に類似する岩屋山式石室になる{{Sfn|峯塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。早い段階で盗掘に遭っているため副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半-中葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。当時としては大王陵クラスの石室が構築された古墳として、奈良県内では代表的な終末期古墳の1つになる{{Sfn|峯塚古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}。付近では大型の終末期古墳として[[塚穴山古墳]]（天理市勾田町、峯塚古墳の先行古墳）も知られるほか、[[奈良時代]]の墓として精巧な[[海獣葡萄鏡]]が副葬された[[杣之内火葬墓]]があり、これらを[[物部氏]]の首長墓とする説が挙げられている[[白石太一郎]] 「古墳からみた物部氏」『古墳の被葬者を推理する（中公叢書）』 中央公論新社、2018年、pp. 253-260。。また[[斉明天皇]]陵の可能性のある岩屋山古墳の石室と同型式である点、本古墳の貼石で使用された天理砂岩の切石は斉明朝の[[酒船石遺跡]]（明日香村）でも使用されている点から、斉明天皇期に活躍した被葬者像が想定される古墳になる日野宏『物部氏の拠点集落 布留遺跡（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」140）』新泉社、2019年、pp. 80-88。。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室パース図}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * 石室全長：11.11メートル * 玄室：長さ4.46メートル、幅2.58メートル（奥壁）・2.60メートル（玄門）、高さ2.4メートル * 羨道：長さ6.65メートル、幅2.28メートル、高さ1.70メートル 石室は[[花崗岩]]の巨石の切石が用いられた整美なものになり、石材のうち大きなものでは幅4.5メートル・高さ1.2メートルを測る。玄室は奥壁・側壁とも2段積みであり、目地には漆喰が使用される{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。玄室の天井石は3枚{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 石室の形態としては[[岩屋山古墳]]（[[明日香村]]）と同様の特徴を有しており、「岩屋山式石室」と捉えられるが、岩屋山古墳石室とは玄室は同規模であるものの羨道は短縮されている{{Sfn|峯塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。また[[小谷古墳]]（[[橿原市]]）石室とはほぼ同一設計になるとして注目される{{Sfn|峯塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}[[河上邦彦]]『飛鳥発掘物語』扶桑社、2004年、pp. 66-69。。前時代（古墳時代後期）には各地域の有力豪族同士で石室が似る例は少ないが、終末期に入ると小谷古墳と峯塚古墳のように地域を超えて同一設計で石室が築造されるようになり、石工集団が豪族管理から朝廷管理に移行した様子が示唆される。 File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|石室俯瞰図 Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|参考：[[小谷古墳]]石室俯瞰図 File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（羨道方向） File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * 史跡説明板（天理市教育委員会、2009年設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=峯塚古墳|title=天理市の文化財|publisher=天理市教育委員会|ref={{Harvid|峯塚古墳（天理市の文化財）|1990}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=峯塚古墳|title=天理の古墳100|publisher=天理市教育委員会|ref={{Harvid|峯塚古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=峯塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|峯塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=桑原久男|year=2014|chapter=峯塚古墳とその周辺|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013 |title=杣之内古墳群の研究 |publisher=杣之内古墳群研究会 |ncid=BB15553252 |id={{全国書誌番号|22415717}} |ref={{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}}} - リンクは全国遺跡報告総覧 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|title=奈良県天理市峯塚古墳・西乗鞍古墳・鑵子塚古墳測量調査報告（天理大学歴史研究会調査研究報告 1）|publisher=天理大学歴史研究会|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=峯塚古墳|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 |title=物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群 |publisher=天理市教育委員会|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連項目 == * [[杣之内古墳群]] ** [[塚穴山古墳]] * [[物部氏]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Minezuka Kofun}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html 峯塚古墳] - 天理市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:みねつかこふん}} [[Category:杣之内古墳群]] '''峯塚古墳'''（みねづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[杣之内古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]東縁、東から延びる小丘陵尾根の南裾に築造された大型円墳である史跡説明板。。[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）に[[天理大学]]歴史研究会による測量調査が実施されているほか、これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形。墳丘は3段築成で、1段目裾は直径35.5メートル、2段目裾は直径28.4メートル、3段目裾は直径17.6メートルを測り、墳丘全体としては高さ約5メートルを測る。墳丘外表では[[凝灰岩]]質[[砂岩]]（天理砂岩）の長方形切石によるレンガ状貼石が認められる。また墳丘周囲には周濠の可能性が指摘される。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。[[花崗岩]]の巨石の切石を用いた整美な大型石室で、[[岩屋山古墳]]（[[明日香村]]）に類似する岩屋山式石室になる{{Sfn|峯塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。早い段階で盗掘に遭っているため副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半-中葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。当時としては大王陵クラスの石室が構築された古墳として、奈良県内では代表的な終末期古墳の1つになる{{Sfn|峯塚古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}。付近では大型の終末期古墳として[[塚穴山古墳]]（天理市勾田町、峯塚古墳の先行古墳）も知られるほか、[[奈良時代]]の墓として精巧な[[海獣葡萄鏡]]が副葬された[[杣之内火葬墓]]があり、これらを[[物部氏]]の首長墓とする説が挙げられている[[白石太一郎]] 「古墳からみた物部氏」『古墳の被葬者を推理する（中公叢書）』 中央公論新社、2018年、pp. 253-260。。また[[斉明天皇]]陵の可能性のある岩屋山古墳の石室と同型式である点、本古墳の貼石で使用された天理砂岩の切石は斉明朝の[[酒船石遺跡]]（明日香村）でも使用されている点から、斉明天皇期に活躍した被葬者像が想定される古墳になる日野宏『物部氏の拠点集落 布留遺跡（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」140）』新泉社、2019年、pp. 80-88。。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室パース図}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * 石室全長：11.11メートル * 玄室：長さ4.46メートル、幅2.58メートル（奥壁）・2.60メートル（玄門）、高さ2.4メートル * 羨道：長さ6.65メートル、幅2.28メートル、高さ1.70メートル 石室は[[花崗岩]]の巨石の切石が用いられた整美なものになり、石材のうち大きなものでは幅4.5メートル・高さ1.2メートルを測る。玄室は奥壁・側壁とも2段積みであり、目地には漆喰が使用される{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。玄室の天井石は3枚{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 石室の形態としては[[岩屋山古墳]]（[[明日香村]]）と同様の特徴を有しており、「岩屋山式石室」と捉えられるが、岩屋山古墳石室とは玄室は同規模であるものの羨道は短縮されている{{Sfn|峯塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。また[[小谷古墳]]（[[橿原市]]）石室とはほぼ同一設計になるとして注目される{{Sfn|峯塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}[[河上邦彦]]『飛鳥発掘物語』扶桑社、2004年、pp. 66-69。。前時代（古墳時代後期）には各地域の有力豪族同士で石室が似る例は少ないが、終末期に入ると小谷古墳と峯塚古墳のように地域を超えて同一設計で石室が築造されるようになり、石工集団が豪族管理から朝廷管理に移行した様子が示唆される。 File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|石室俯瞰図 Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|参考：[[小谷古墳]]石室俯瞰図 File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（羨道方向） File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Motoyama{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} | part_of = {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanochi Town, Mine-dō{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}} | coordinates = 34| coordinates = 35| coordinates = 38.25 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 50| coordinates = 57.27 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Circular mound | area = Diameter 35.5m
Height 5m | burial = {{ill|Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Iwayayama type) | burial goods = | Associated Mounds = | built = Early to mid-7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb = | Historic Site = None | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = Circular mound | map_dot_label = Minezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = Top }} '''Minezuka Kofun''' (峯塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. The Kofun, shaped as a circular mound, is part of the {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and is not designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The kofun is a large circular Kofun built at the southern foot of a small hill ridge extending east from the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}.Explanatory board at the site. A survey was conducted by the {{ill|Tenri University History Research Society|ja|天理大学歴史研究会}} in 1969, but no excavation has been carried out to date. The mound is circular, built in three tiers. The first tier measures 35.5 meters in diameter, the second tier 28.4 meters, and the third tier 17.6 meters. The mound stands approximately 5 meters tall. The outer surface of the mound features rectangular blocks of tuff sandstone (Tenri sandstone), giving it a brick-like appearance. There is also speculation about the presence of a surrounding moat. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. This meticulously constructed large chamber uses cut stones of granite, resembling the chamber of {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka Village|ja|明日香村}} and is classified as an Iwayayama-type chamber. However, the burial goods remain unclear due to early looting. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}. It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡}} further indicate the influence of the reign of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇}}. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳}}, leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] -
