Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|12=draft}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=draft}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}}.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|12=draft}} further indicate the influence of the reign of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|12=draft}}. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}}, leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=draft}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - Tenri City Official Website {{citation needed|Minezuka Kofun}} {{citation needed|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯塚古墳|version=100256751|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanochi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Burial sites of the Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minezuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯塚古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Minezuka Kofun in Habikino City, Osaka Prefecture|Minekazuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft}}s | name = Minezuka Kofun | image = [[File:Minezuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Motoyama{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} | part_of = {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft}} | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|qq=draft}}, Somanochi Town, Mine-dō{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}} | coordinates = 34| coordinates = 35| coordinates = 38.25 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 50| coordinates = 57.27 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Circular mound | area = Diameter 35.5m
Height 5m | burial = {{ill|Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}} (Iwayayama type) | burial goods = | Associated Mounds = | built = Early to mid-7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb = | Historic Site = None | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = Circular mound | map_dot_label = Minezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = Top }} '''Minezuka Kofun''' (峯塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|qq=draft}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. The Kofun, shaped as a circular mound, is part of the {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft}} and is not designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The kofun is a large circular Kofun built at the southern foot of a small hill ridge extending east from the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft}}.Explanatory board at the site. A survey was conducted by the {{ill|Tenri University History Research Society|ja|天理大学歴史研究会|qq=draft}} in 1969, but no excavation has been carried out to date. The mound is circular, built in three tiers. The first tier measures 35.5 meters in diameter, the second tier 28.4 meters, and the third tier 17.6 meters. The mound stands approximately 5 meters tall. The outer surface of the mound features rectangular blocks of tuff sandstone (Tenri sandstone), giving it a brick-like appearance. There is also speculation about the presence of a surrounding moat. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. This meticulously constructed large chamber uses cut stones of granite, resembling the chamber of {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka Village|ja|明日香村|qq=draft}} and is classified as an Iwayayama-type chamber. However, the burial goods remain unclear due to early looting. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft}}. It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|qq=draft}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft}}.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|qq=draft}} further indicate the influence of the reign of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|qq=draft}}. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|qq=draft}}, leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - Tenri City Official Website {{citation needed|Minezuka Kofun}} {{citation needed|Minezuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯塚古墳|version=100256751|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanochi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Burial sites of the Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minezuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯塚古墳]]

Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Motoyama{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} | part_of = {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanochi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri City|qq=}}, Somanochi Town, Mine-dō{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}} | coordinates = 34| coordinates = 35| coordinates = 38.25 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 50| coordinates = 57.27 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Circular mound | area = Diameter 35.5m
Height 5m | burial = {{ill|Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama type) | burial goods = | Associated Mounds = | built = Early to mid-7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb = | Historic Site = None | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = Circular mound | map_dot_label = Minezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = Top }} '''Minezuka Kofun''' (峯塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri City|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. The Kofun, shaped as a circular mound, is part of the {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanochi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=}} and is not designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The kofun is a large circular Kofun built at the southern foot of a small hill ridge extending east from the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Basin|qq=}}.Explanatory board at the site. A survey was conducted by the {{ill|Tenri University History Research Society|ja|天理大学歴史研究会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri University History Research Society|qq=}} in 1969, but no excavation has been carried out to date. The mound is circular, built in three tiers. The first tier measures 35.5 meters in diameter, the second tier 28.4 meters, and the third tier 17.6 meters. The mound stands approximately 5 meters tall. The outer surface of the mound features rectangular blocks of tuff sandstone (Tenri sandstone), giving it a brick-like appearance. There is also speculation about the presence of a surrounding moat. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. This meticulously constructed large chamber uses cut stones of granite, resembling the chamber of {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwayayama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Asuka Village|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Village|qq=}} and is classified as an Iwayayama-type chamber. However, the burial goods remain unclear due to early looting. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana-yama Kofun|qq=}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanochi Cremation Tomb|qq=}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}}.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakafuneishi Site|qq=}} further indicate the influence of the reign of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Saimei|qq=}}. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwayayama Kofun|qq=}}, leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanochi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=}} ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaanayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - Height 5m | burial = {{ill|Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama type) | burial goods = | Associated Mounds = | built = Early to mid-7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb = | Historic Site = None | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = Circular mound | map_dot_label = Minezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = Top }} '''Minezuka Kofun''' (峯塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri City|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. The Kofun, shaped as a circular mound, is part of the {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanochi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=}} and is not designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The kofun is a large circular Kofun built at the southern foot of a small hill ridge extending east from the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Basin|qq=}}.Explanatory board at the site. A survey was conducted by the {{ill|Tenri University History Research Society|ja|天理大学歴史研究会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri University History Research Society|qq=}} in 1969, but no excavation has been carried out to date. The mound is circular, built in three tiers. The first tier measures 35.5 meters in diameter, the second tier 28.4 meters, and the third tier 17.6 meters. The mound stands approximately 5 meters tall. The outer surface of the mound features rectangular blocks of tuff sandstone (Tenri sandstone), giving it a brick-like appearance. There is also speculation about the presence of a surrounding moat. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. This meticulously constructed large chamber uses cut stones of granite, resembling the chamber of {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwayayama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Asuka Village|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Village|qq=}} and is classified as an Iwayayama-type chamber. However, the burial goods remain unclear due to early looting. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana-yama Kofun|qq=}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanochi Cremation Tomb|qq=}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}}.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakafuneishi Site|qq=}} further indicate the influence of the reign of {{ill|Empress Saimei|ja|斉明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Saimei|qq=}}. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwayayama Kofun|qq=}}, leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kodani Kofun|lt=Kodani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kodani Kofun|lt=Kodani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanochi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanochi Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qq=}} ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaanayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - Tenri City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯塚古墳|version=100256751|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanochi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Burial sites of the Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minezuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯塚古墳]]

Height 5m | burial = {{ill|Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama type) | burial goods = | Associated Mounds = | built = Early to mid-7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb = | Historic Site = None | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = Circular mound | map_dot_label = Minezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = Top }} '''Minezuka Kofun''' (峯塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Nara Prefecture, Japan. The Kofun, shaped as a circular mound, is part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] and is not designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The kofun is a large circular Kofun built at the southern foot of a small hill ridge extending east from the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]].Explanatory board at the site. A survey was conducted by the {{ill|Tenri University History Research Society|ja|天理大学歴史研究会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri University History Research Society|qq=}} in 1969, but no excavation has been carried out to date. The mound is circular, built in three tiers. The first tier measures 35.5 meters in diameter, the second tier 28.4 meters, and the third tier 17.6 meters. The mound stands approximately 5 meters tall. The outer surface of the mound features rectangular blocks of tuff sandstone (Tenri sandstone), giving it a brick-like appearance. There is also speculation about the presence of a surrounding moat. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. This meticulously constructed large chamber uses cut stones of granite, resembling the chamber of [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]] and is classified as an Iwayayama-type chamber. However, the burial goods remain unclear due to early looting. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanochi Cremation Tomb|qq=}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakafuneishi Site|qq=}} further indicate the influence of the reign of [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]]. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]], leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kodani Kofun|lt=Kodani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kodani Kofun|lt=Kodani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaanayama Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana-yama Kofun|qq=}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanochi Cremation Tomb|qq=}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakafuneishi Site|qq=}} further indicate the influence of the reign of [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]]. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]], leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kodani Kofun|lt=Kodani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kodani Kofun|lt=Kodani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaanayama Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Minezuka Kofun in Habikino City, Osaka Prefecture|Minekazuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}s | name = Minezuka Kofun | image = [[File:Minezuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Motoyama{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} | part_of = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanochi Town, Mine-dō{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}} | coordinates = 34| coordinates = 35| coordinates = 38.25 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 50| coordinates = 57.27 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Circular mound | area = Diameter 35.5m
Height 5m | burial = {{ill|Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama type) | burial goods = | Associated Mounds = | built = Early to mid-7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb = | Historic Site = None | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = Circular mound | map_dot_label = Minezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = Top }} '''Minezuka Kofun''' (峯塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Nara Prefecture, Japan. The Kofun, shaped as a circular mound, is part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] and is not designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The kofun is a large circular Kofun built at the southern foot of a small hill ridge extending east from the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]].Explanatory board at the site. A survey was conducted by the {{ill|Tenri University History Research Society|ja|天理大学歴史研究会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri University History Research Society|qq=}} in 1969, but no excavation has been carried out to date. The mound is circular, built in three tiers. The first tier measures 35.5 meters in diameter, the second tier 28.4 meters, and the third tier 17.6 meters. The mound stands approximately 5 meters tall. The outer surface of the mound features rectangular blocks of tuff sandstone (Tenri sandstone), giving it a brick-like appearance. There is also speculation about the presence of a surrounding moat. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. This meticulously constructed large chamber uses cut stones of granite, resembling the chamber of [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]] and is classified as an Iwayayama-type chamber. However, the burial goods remain unclear due to early looting. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana-yama Kofun|qq=}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanochi Cremation Tomb|qq=}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakafuneishi Site|qq=}} further indicate the influence of the reign of [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]]. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]], leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kodani Kofun|lt=Kodani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kodani Kofun|lt=Kodani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaanayama Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Minezuka Kofun in Habikino City, Osaka Prefecture|Minekazuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}s | name = Minezuka Kofun | image = [[File:Minezuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Motoyama{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} | part_of = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanochi Town, Mine-dō{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}} | coordinates = 34| coordinates = 35| coordinates = 38.25 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 50| coordinates = 57.27 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Circular mound | area = Diameter 35.5m
Height 5m | burial = {{ill|Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama type) | burial goods = | Associated Mounds = | built = Early to mid-7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb = | Historic Site = None | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = Circular mound | map_dot_label = Minezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = Top }} '''Minezuka Kofun''' (峯塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Nara Prefecture, Japan. The Kofun, shaped as a circular mound, is part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] and is not designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The kofun is a large circular Kofun built at the southern foot of a small hill ridge extending east from the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]].Explanatory board at the site. A survey was conducted by the {{ill|Tenri University History Research Society|ja|天理大学歴史研究会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri University History Research Society|qq=}} in 1969, but no excavation has been carried out to date. The mound is circular, built in three tiers. The first tier measures 35.5 meters in diameter, the second tier 28.4 meters, and the third tier 17.6 meters. The mound stands approximately 5 meters tall. The outer surface of the mound features rectangular blocks of tuff sandstone (Tenri sandstone), giving it a brick-like appearance. There is also speculation about the presence of a surrounding moat. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. This meticulously constructed large chamber uses cut stones of granite, resembling the chamber of [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]] and is classified as an Iwayayama-type chamber. However, the burial goods remain unclear due to early looting. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana-yama Kofun|qq=}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanochi Cremation Tomb|qq=}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakafuneishi Site|qq=}} further indicate the influence of the reign of [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]]. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]], leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kodani Kofun|lt=Kodani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kodani Kofun|lt=Kodani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaanayama Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - Height 5m | burial = {{ill|Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama type) | burial goods = | Associated Mounds = | built = Early to mid-7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb = | Historic Site = None | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = Circular mound | map_dot_label = Minezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = Top }} '''Minezuka Kofun''' (峯塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Nara Prefecture, Japan. The Kofun, shaped as a circular mound, is part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] and is not designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The kofun is a large circular Kofun built at the southern foot of a small hill ridge extending east from the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]].Explanatory board at the site. A survey was conducted by the {{ill|Tenri University History Research Society|ja|天理大学歴史研究会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri University History Research Society|qq=}} in 1969, but no excavation has been carried out to date. The mound is circular, built in three tiers. The first tier measures 35.5 meters in diameter, the second tier 28.4 meters, and the third tier 17.6 meters. The mound stands approximately 5 meters tall. The outer surface of the mound features rectangular blocks of tuff sandstone (Tenri sandstone), giving it a brick-like appearance. There is also speculation about the presence of a surrounding moat. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. This meticulously constructed large chamber uses cut stones of granite, resembling the chamber of [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]] and is classified as an Iwayayama-type chamber. However, the burial goods remain unclear due to early looting. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana-yama Kofun|qq=}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanochi Cremation Tomb|qq=}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakafuneishi Site|qq=}} further indicate the influence of the reign of [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]]. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]], leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kodani Kofun|lt=Kodani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kodani Kofun|lt=Kodani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaanayama Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - Tenri City Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|峯塚古墳|version=100256751|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanochi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Burial sites of the Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Minezuka Kofun]] [[ja:峯塚古墳]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:杣之内古墳群]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]]

Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana-yama Kofun|qq=}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanochi Cremation Tomb|qq=}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakafuneishi Site|qq=}} further indicate the influence of the reign of [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]]. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]], leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kodani Kofun|lt=Kodani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kodani Kofun|lt=Kodani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaanayama Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Minezuka Kofun in Habikino City, Osaka Prefecture|Minekazuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}s | name = Minezuka Kofun | image = [[File:Minezuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Motoyama{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} | part_of = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanochi Town, Mine-dō{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}} | coordinates = 34| coordinates = 35| coordinates = 38.25 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 50| coordinates = 57.27 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Circular mound | area = Diameter 35.5m
Height 5m | burial = {{ill|Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama type) | burial goods = | Associated Mounds = | built = Early to mid-7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb = | Historic Site = None | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = Circular mound | map_dot_label = Minezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = Top }} '''Minezuka Kofun''' (峯塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Nara Prefecture, Japan. The Kofun, shaped as a circular mound, is part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] and is not designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The kofun is a large circular Kofun built at the southern foot of a small hill ridge extending east from the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]].Explanatory board at the site. A survey was conducted by the {{ill|Tenri University History Research Society|ja|天理大学歴史研究会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri University History Research Society|qq=}} in 1969, but no excavation has been carried out to date. The mound is circular, built in three tiers. The first tier measures 35.5 meters in diameter, the second tier 28.4 meters, and the third tier 17.6 meters. The mound stands approximately 5 meters tall. The outer surface of the mound features rectangular blocks of tuff sandstone (Tenri sandstone), giving it a brick-like appearance. There is also speculation about the presence of a surrounding moat. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. This meticulously constructed large chamber uses cut stones of granite, resembling the chamber of [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]] and is classified as an Iwayayama-type chamber. However, the burial goods remain unclear due to early looting. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana-yama Kofun|qq=}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanochi Cremation Tomb|qq=}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakafuneishi Site|qq=}} further indicate the influence of the reign of [[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Empress Saimei]]. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]], leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kodani Kofun|lt=Kodani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kodani Kofun|lt=Kodani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaanayama Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kodani Kofun|lt=Kodani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaanayama Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] -
