Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Motoyama{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}} | part_of = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri City|1=Tenri, Nara}}, Somanochi Town, Mine-dō{{Sfn|Minezuka Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}} | coordinates = 34| coordinates = 35| coordinates = 38.25 | coordinates = 135| coordinates = 50| coordinates = 57.27 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Circular mound | area = Diameter 35.5m
Height 5m | burial = {{ill|Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama type) | burial goods = | Associated Mounds = | built = Early to mid-7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb = | Historic Site = None | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = Circular mound | map_dot_label = Minezuka Kofun | map_dot_label = Top }} '''Minezuka Kofun''' (峯塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri City|1=Tenri, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. The Kofun, shaped as a circular mound, is part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} and is not designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The kofun is a large circular Kofun built at the southern foot of a small hill ridge extending east from the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}.Explanatory board at the site. A survey was conducted by the {{ill|Tenri University History Research Society|ja|天理大学歴史研究会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri University History Research Society|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1969, but no excavation has been carried out to date. The mound is circular, built in three tiers. The first tier measures 35.5 meters in diameter, the second tier 28.4 meters, and the third tier 17.6 meters. The mound stands approximately 5 meters tall. The outer surface of the mound features rectangular blocks of tuff sandstone (Tenri sandstone), giving it a brick-like appearance. There is also speculation about the presence of a surrounding moat. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. This meticulously constructed large chamber uses cut stones of granite, resembling the chamber of {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|en|Iwayayama Kofun|WD=Q11474389|lt=Iwayayama Kofun|1=Iwayayama Kofun}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka Village|1=Asuka, Nara}} and is classified as an Iwayayama-type chamber. However, the burial goods remain unclear due to early looting. The kofun is estimated to date back to the early to mid-7th century during the {{ill|Late Kofun Period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana-yama Kofun|qq=}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanochi Cremation Tomb|qq=}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}}.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakafuneishi Site|qq=}} further indicate the influence of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|en|Empress Kōgyoku|WD=Q235296|lt=Empress Saimei|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|en|Iwayayama Kofun|WD=Q11474389|lt=Iwayayama Kofun|1=Iwayayama Kofun}}, leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kotani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kotani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|WD=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaanayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] - It is considered one of the most representative late kofun in Nara Prefecture and was constructed with a stone chamber comparable to those in imperial Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the large {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaanayama Kofun|lt=Tsukaana-yama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana-yama Kofun|qq=}}, a predecessor of Minezuka Kofun. Additionally, the sophisticated {{ill|Somanochi Cremation Tomb|ja|杣之内火葬墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somanochi Cremation Tomb|qq=}}, featuring a {{ill|Sea-beast grape mirror|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-beast grape mirror|qq=}}, suggests these mounds may have belonged to the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|qid=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}}.{{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018|pages=253–260}} The similarities in construction material and technique with the {{ill|Sakafuneishi Site|ja|酒船石遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakafuneishi Site|qq=}} further indicate the influence of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|en|Empress Kōgyoku|qid=Q235296|lt=Empress Saimei|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}. == burial == [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective drawing of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout drawing of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve horizontal stone chamber that opens to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows:{{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}: * Total Length: 11.11 meters * Burial Chamber: Length 4.46 meters, Width 2.58 meters (rear wall) / 2.60 meters (entrance), Height 2.4 meters * Passage: Length 6.65 meters, Width 2.28 meters, Height 1.70 meters The chamber, constructed with granite cut stones, features blocks as large as 4.5 meters in width and 1.2 meters in height. Both the rear and side walls of the chamber are built in two tiers, with plaster used to seal the joints. The ceiling of the chamber consists of three stones. The chamber shares characteristics with the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|en|Iwayayama Kofun|qid=Q11474389|lt=Iwayayama Kofun|1=Iwayayama Kofun}}, leading to its classification as an "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." The chamber area align closely with the {{ill|Kotani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}}, indicating shared construction designs. This suggests a shift in stonemason management from regional clans to the imperial court during the Late Kofun Period.{{Cite book|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|title=Discovering Asuka|year=2004|pages=66–69}} File:Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Reference: Overhead view of {{ill|Kotani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|lt=Kotani Kofun|ja|小谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotani Kofun|qq=}} stone chamber File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) File:Minezuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the passage) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (toward the entrance) File:Minezuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Explanatory board by the Tenri City Board of Education, 2009 * {{Cite book|author=|title=Tenri City Historic Site|year=1990|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=100 Kofuns of Tenri|year=2015|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education}} * {{Cite book|author=Kuwahara Hisao|title=Research on the Somanochi Kofun Group|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.74013}} * {{Cite book|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|title=Understanding Kofun Period Burials (Chūōkōron Series)|year=2018}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qid=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Tsukaanayama Kofun|ja|塚穴山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaanayama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|qid=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321686880.html Minezuka Kofun] -
